1156.name: Dark Cave
1156.description: You stand in a tight rock corridor, starved of air and the nurturing rays of the sun. The icy winds howl menacingly from the mouth of the cavern, flurries of snow whirling into the darkness.

1157.name: Narrow Tunnel
1157.description: The air is stale and rank, and the only sound you can make out is the panting of your own breath. White sparks flash before your eyes as you begin to suffocate...

1158.name: Before the Golden Door
1158.description: You bathe in the rich glow of an enormous arched door that reflects the light pouring through a shaft in the rock. Shaped of beaten gold, the reliefs upon it picture a fertile valley nestled in the icy reaches of the Northern Wastes. A tall stand of valerian sways gently, its pink flowers releasing a pungent odor.

1159.name: Head of the Valley
1159.description: You stand at the very head of a hidden valley, bounded on all sides by the steep rocky slopes of the mountains. The icy winds that would chill the bone at this latitude have been beaten back, and the air is warm and unruffled. The view from this lofty height is breathtaking, letting you survey the whole valley in all its majesty.

1160.name: Rocky Road
1160.description: Down from the head of the valley leads a narrow rocky road, which is perched delicately on the valley walls. You stumble and barely maintain your balance on the uneven surface.

npcs.name: Peryton
npcs.description: blue feathered wings.  Oddly enough, it casts the shadow of a man.

1162.name: Green Fields
1162.description: A wide expanse of grass, softer and greener than anything you have seen before, stretches along the valley floor, divided by low drystone walls that are covered in moss.

npcs.name: Sheep
npcs.description: alter that perception.

1163.name: Barn
1163.description: Inside the barn the darkness is broken only by the occasional shaft of sunlight that streams down from holes in the tiled roof. Abandoned for some time, the air is filled with a musty, neglected smell.

1164.name: By the Plunge Pool
1164.description: The white hail pouring down from the cascade falls into a plunge pool, churning and frothing noisily. Scoured out by swirling rocks, it is impossible to tell how deep it actually is due to the dense white foam.

npcs.name: Auburn otter
npcs.description: and surfacing with complete, joyful abandon.

1166.name: River Bluehorn
1166.description: The Bluehorn flows out of a large cave mouth, tripping over a series of boulders that line its course down the steep slope. The swirling eddies rock your boat violently, but you maintain your balance.

npcs.name: Auburn otter
npcs.description: and surfacing with complete, joyful abandon.

1167.name: Into the Caves
1167.description: The Bluehorn tumbles down from a wide crack in the roof of this cave, the meltwater from glaciers far above you. Suddenly drenched by the bitterly cold water, you gasp in shock. Remembrance of the Zephyr is no longer on cooldown!

1168.name: Black Cave
1168.description: The darkness is thick and impenetrable, at odds with everything else in this valley. Sulphurous fumes rise from bubbling hot water on the cave walls, making you wrinkle your nose in disgust.

npcs.name: Silver dragon
npcs.description: creature must be the last remnant of what populated the valley before the

1169.name: River Bluehorn
1169.description: As the river flows into the forest, trees cluster closely by, their roots supporting the banks. Willows drape their long branches into the clear water, obscuring the path ahead with thick greenery.

1170.name: Fording the Bluehorn
1170.description: The river abruptly becomes very shallow, the pebbles and gravel of its bed covered by ankle-deep water. Its west bank marks the edge of a forest, vines that dangle overhead brushing lightly at you.

1171.name: River Bluehorn
1171.description: The river flows down towards the valley floor, moving out of the forest into open countryside. Your boat bobs up and down on the silvery water, whirling around small islands in the river's course that are planted with birch trees.

1172.name: River Bluehorn
1172.description: The river water is darker and slower now, heavy with silt it has swept up from the plains on the valley floor. Meandering gently around steep banks thickly sown with crops, the river is becoming noticeable wider.

1173.name: River Bluehorn
1173.description: The river is gentler and less choppy than further up its course, flowing slowly by a tall stone wall to the north. The vines that trail from small gaps almost completely cover the stonework, binding it more strongly than mortar.

1174.name: Swirling Confluence
1174.description: A wide aqueduct has been built through the city above, running down the embankment to the river. Where the rivers meet, the water boils and churns, great clouds of steam rising upwards and obscuring your vision.

1180.name: In the Hot Spring
1180.description: This is the largest of the pools, the water a dull blue and saturated with healing minerals. You can feel all your cares being leeched away by the relaxing warmth.

1181.name: Wooden Platform
1181.description: Some craftsmen have laid wooden slats across posts driven into the hot pools below, creating a wide wooden deck. Amid the curls of steam that rise from between the boards, you can see Teraleans dangling their feet into the waters below.

1182.name: Wooden Steps
1182.description: Wooden boards make up a rough staircase that climbs up the slope of the valley to the north. Mounted on stilts above the rough terrain below, the stairway makes climbing the slope relatively easy.

1183.name: Forested Slope
1183.description: Tufts of green ferns and reeds poke up from the banks of small watercourses that flow down between the oak trees. The silence is broken only by the occasional rustle of foliage and the crunch of fallen leaves under your feet.

1184.name: Coppice
1184.description: The northern forests have been separated from the pine expanse to the southwest since the hot springs erupted from the ground. The trees are taller and neater, their leaves bathed in sunlight. This small cluster of trees is the perfect place to rest awhile.

1186.name: Private Spring
1186.description: This pool of steaming water is slightly separated from the rest, screened on all sides by tall boulders. Now the private leisure area of a Teralean noble, coloured glass tiles cover the edges of the pool.

npcs.name: Lord Herrin
npcs.description: Perrinas grows older and more decrepit, his bride seeks pleasure elsewhere.

npcs.name: Queen Irisa
npcs.description: Teralea.  Unfortunately, so are her 'encounters' with other men.  She swims through the hot spring, naked as the day she was born.

1187.name: End of the Valley
1187.description: You have reached the very end of Teralea, the forested slopes closing in on this point and meeting in a narrow gully. Above you there are mountains taller than any you have seen before, ice and snow covering their peaks. The warm wind is trapped and swirls around you.

1189.name: Forgotten Clearing
1189.description: Deep within this utterly silent forest, you come across a circular clearing. A strange ring of stones is at the centre, hewn boulders scattered all around. Ivy and vines have almost completely engulfed the area, tall birch trees surrounding you on every side. Not a sound can be heard.

npcs.name: Guardian of Teralea
npcs.description: over the valley below.  His gaze takes in everything that is happening in his domain.  This must be the spirit that first led people to this warm,

1191.name: Above the Hot Spring
1191.description: A rough path runs above the hot springs, keeping close to the valley wall. A small garden has been sown here, and a few benches have been placed around the lawn. A few people are resting here, enjoying the view of the valley.

1192.name: Rock Lip
1192.description: A high ledge of rock overlooks the hot spring area. From atop this slippery rock face you can see all the pools of hot water and crowds of people blurring in and out of the clouds of steam.

1193.name: Gravel Pathway
1193.description: This roadway has been covered in a thick layer of gravel, the various colors separated out into swirls and criss-crossing patterns. The effect is so pleasing it almost seems a shame to walk along the path. Best keep to the grass verge.

1194.name: Rocky Scramble
1194.description: The valley floor rises suddenly, climbing up and out of the dense forest below. The hillside is littered with fallen boulders covered in growths of moss and lichen. Dull gray tin flecks are scattered through the stone.

1195.name: In the Ferns
1195.description: As the terrain rises from the flat valley floor, the forest recedes into ferns and isolated pines. Walls of mist roll down the slope, shrouding what lies beyond from your eyes.

1196.name: Deep Forest
1196.description: The light that trickles through the layers of leaves above you is a faint dappled green. The oak and sycamore trees press tightly around you and the only sound is the faint chirruping of a bird somewhere close at hand.

1198.name: Clearing
1198.description: A small gap in the canopy of trees allows light to flood down onto you from above. Half-rotten wood and tree roots jut out of the ground, all carpeted undergrowth.

1199.name: Unicorn Paddock
1199.description: The cool depths of the forest, bathed in the mist that flows down from the north, are home to the unicorns of Teralea. A low stone wall surrounds the clearing, which is home to a single family.

npcs.name: Unicorn foal
npcs.description: The foal nuzzles close to you, harrumphing peacefully.

npcs.name: Injured unicorn
npcs.description: utter sadness.

1201.name: Edge of the Forest
1201.description: The western slopes of the valley are thickly forested with oak and pine, separated from the eastern farmland by the fast-flowing Bluehorn. The woods are lush here, ferns and ivy surrounding you.

1202.name: Along the Path
1202.description: The road is joined by several smaller paths that lead in all directions, the happy confusion only increased by the mounted travellers and carts moving in all directions.

1203.name: Storehouse
1203.description: Inside this stone tower mountains of grain are resting on wooden pallets. There are several more floors above you, each bowing under the weight of yet more grain.

1204.name: Stone Bridge
1204.description: A wide bridge crosses the Bluehorn in a single graceful arch, the smooth stone blocks carved with detailed reliefs of ancient kings. The roadway is inlaid with a map of the valley, rendered in bronze. You are getting thirsty.

1205.name: Amber Road
1205.description: The road is now passing through the very centre of the valley, becoming wider and busier with every step you take. Tall iron posts support a smooth amber spheres which glow a soft, suffuse light.

1206.name: Amber Road
1206.description: The valley floor begins to rise again to the north, climbing towards a high plateau to the northwest. Over the tree-tops you can make out a bustling city protected by tall embankments on every side. The amber spheres flare gently as you pass.

1207.name: Amber Road
1207.description: The Amber Road becomes a wide crossroads here, paths leading in every direction. A rough worn road travels up a rocky incline to the west, mists rolling down it. To the east you can see the road climb up a tall stone embankment towards a city in the distance.

1208.name: Amber Road
1208.description: The Amber Road enters a bustling city, the mercantile heart of Teralea. The tall, narrow buildings cluster around the road, their roofs swamped with gables and towers. Running above the road is an aqueduct of hot water.

1209.name: Teralea Square
1209.description: This is the heart of urban Teralea, a wide space of smooth stone surrounded by a colonnade of veined granite. The impressive facades of noblemen's houses around the square's perimeter are covered in growths of copper ivy. Over the rooftops to the east you can see the glittering dome of a vast temple, and a road to the north climbs further up the valley walls to a palace, perched on a ledge above.

1210.name: The Ivory Temple
1210.description: It is hard to believe that this pillared hall is actually indoors. Thick pillars of ivory support an enormous arched roof of glass and crystal, so clear and well-cleaned that it is only just visible. Small braziers of glowing coals line the aisle towards the altar.

npcs.name: Athlerim the High Priest
npcs.description: king's health waning, he has taken on a more practical role in running Teralea and its people, a task he carries out with care and diligence.

1211.name: Meditation Chamber
1211.description: This small chamber off the main hall of the temple is delightfully cool, the windows to the south thrown open to the wind. Delicate scents rise from an array of coloured candles and incense bowls.

npcs.name: Venrim the Priest
npcs.description: and peaceful.  He is at one with the world around him.

1212.name: The High Altar
1212.description: The dais you walk upon is dominated by a simple altar of black marble, the colour in contrast to everything else in this temple. The panels on each side are decorated with inscriptions in a language you cannot read, and atop the altar is a tiny chalice of ivory.

1213.name: Sacristy
1213.description: This small chamber off the main altar is where the clergy store their special robes and vestments. The many wooden shelves hold golden treasures from the first years of settlement in the valley. Some bear the same writing you saw on the altar.

npcs.name: Beinas
npcs.description: dust you off as well.

1214.name: Palace Roadway
1214.description: A steep roadway zigzags haphazardly through the city, climbing up a steep incline towards the palace. Tall columns mark the entrance to a magnificent courtyard, inscribed with the arms of the king of Teralea.

1215.name: Palace Courtyard
1215.description: The courtyard is surrounded by a colonnade on two sides, the tall gabled roofs of the palace above them. Over the western wall a cliff drops towards the city below, spires of smoke rising heavenwards. A small fountain burbles happily.

npcs.name: Courtier
npcs.description: tradition.

1216.name: Banqueting Hall
1216.description: One enormous room, this hall with its ornate trestle tables could seat practically every Teralean in one go. The dais at the south end of the room is furnished with gilt chairs, wall-paintings depicting the history of Teralea.

npcs.name: Lady Naeras
npcs.description: measure.  She will prove a far better queen than the current one, no doubt.

1217.name: Throne Chamber
1217.description: This circular room is adorned with velvet drapes of deep crimson bearing the symbol of Perrinas in gold thread. Wide steps lead up to a throne of ebony and stone, which looks as ancient as the chalice in the temple.

1218.name: Alabaster Hall
1218.description: The long hall is lined with ornamental jeralah trees, pruned into delicate spirals. The long avenue down towards the throne chamber is marked with a red carpet, and enormous candle-bearing chandeliers hang down from the ceiling.

npcs.name: Prince Naeras
npcs.description: all right.

1219.name: Oaken Balcony
1219.description: This wide balcony overlooks the whole of the valley, the view unobstructed by the towers of the palace to the east. Below you the domes of the city give way to the forests of the northern slopes, clouds of mist rising from the rocky plateau of the hot springs.

npcs.name: King Perrinas
npcs.description: Teralea now beyond him.  His loyal friends Feltas and Athlerim are now the

1222.name: Along the Path
1222.description: Running along the bottom of the valley floor is a wide road of stone, lined with low jeralah bushes. All around you people in simple tunics chatter and laugh, moving only to avoid speeding wagons.

1223.name: Corn Fields
1223.description: You stand in the middle of an enormous field of corn, a bracing wind ruffling the stalks in long rippling waves. The fat golden ears are almost ripe, the farm hands checking them for quality.

npcs.name: Farm hand
npcs.description: mask of animal skin is most likely to spare the sweaty half-elf the

1224.name: Corn Fields
1224.description: You stand in the middle of an enormous field of corn, a bracing wind ruffling the stalks in long rippling waves. The occasional poppy waves in the breeze, but otherwise there are no weeds here.

npcs.name: Farm hand
npcs.description: mask of animal skin is most likely to spare the sweaty half-elf the

1225.name: Paddock
1225.description: A wide green meadow lies enclosed in low wooden fences. Several bay horses graze quietly, watched over by a farm hand. They harrumph as you draw near but otherwise tolerate your presence.

1226.name: Paddy Field
1226.description: The farmer's children tend the green shoots in this field, the brown waters only knee-deep. The heat is merciless, but they seem happy enough in their silk tunics and wide straw hats.

1228.name: Orchard
1228.description: Irrigation channels run in between regimented lines of fruit trees, their branches bending under the weight of so much produce. A few of the fruits are familiar to you, but several which shimmer with strange colours seem juicier than any you have encountered.

1229.name: Neat Lawns
1229.description: Atop the same plateau the farmhouse is built upon is a wide green lawn, the grass kept neat and short by a goat which has been tethered to a post in the centre of the lawn. The garden is surrounded by a low border of ferns.

1230.name: Outhouse
1230.description: This small wooden cabin houses the various tools a farmer might need. Some of these look fairly benign, but there are others which look like they're for removing particularly stubborn calves from birthing heifers.

1231.name: Inside a Farmhouse
1231.description: The farmhouse is a single room with an enormous hearth at one end. A large oaken table is covered with pewter platters and barrels of honey mead have been piled up. Simple tapestries adorn the walls.

1232.name: Paddy Field
1232.description: The paddy field comes to an end in a series of stone steps that lead out of the cool water. Sloshing out of the farelith-filled pool, you come across a small plateau with a brick farmhouse atop it.

npcs.name: Dailas
npcs.description: entirely focused on the stubby green stalks that poke up through the water. The farelith plant is a staple foodstuff - no wonder it's so important to him.

1233.name: Paddy Field
1233.description: Partially covered by landslide debris, the brown waters lap gently at small green shoots that poke tentatively skywards. The rippling water sparkles and reflects your face as you wade by. A patch of vibrant green watercress grows here, its leaves crisp and fresh.

1234.name: Rocky Ledge
1234.description: A great ridge of rock prevents water escaping from a shallow lake to the east. Several cows peer at you, very bemused, before returning to their grazing.

1235.name: End of the Road
1235.description: The road should continue along the valley wall, but a recent landslide has buried it completely. The pile of slumped rock and earth shivers as you step cautiously along it.

1175.name: Warm Brook
1175.description: The stream of warm water flows in an artificial channel of stone, raised to the level of the surrounding roofs by tall arches. Several people shout angrily at you, obviously for travelling along their source of hot water.

npcs.name: Brook trout
npcs.description: Its dark green scales are patterned with blue rings which flash brightly

1176.name: Warm Brook
1176.description: The natural flow of hot water away from the springs has been diverted into an aqueduct that runs into the city. Peering down, you can see the people's astonished faces as you shoot past. Several children laugh and point.

npcs.name: Brook trout
npcs.description: Its dark green scales are patterned with blue rings which flash brightly

1178.name: Hot Water Cascade
1178.description: The warm water that comes to the surface in the springs flows down over a ridge of rock, throwing great sheets of spray into the air. Ferns grow thickly along the gentle slope that leads up to the springs, clustering around the cascade.

npcs.name: Citizen of teralea
npcs.description: you pass by.

1179.name: The Hot Springs
1179.description: All round you swirl clouds of water vapour which rise from pools of bubbling water. The ferns have receded, creating a plateau strewn with rocks. Rivulets and streams of hot water froth and churn ceaselessly, showering you with droplets.

npcs.name: Citizen of teralea
npcs.description: you pass by.

1197.name: Hunting Lodge
1197.description: The walls of this wooden building have been decorated with the kills the huntsman has accomplished. A wild boar roasts over a roaring fire and someone has cracked open a barrel of honey mead.

1221.name: Council Chamber
1221.description: In this secluded tower is the meeting place of the Teralean Ruling Council, the nobles and wise men that help govern this valley. A round oaken table is surrounded by tall ornate chairs and is scattered with paperwork. A guttering candle is the only light.

npcs.name: Councillor Feltas
npcs.description: one of the most powerful men in Teralea.
